steps.title: Identify the boundary between list items
steps.content: The sentence lists pivotal events after a colon: the founding of the American GI Forum (needing a year) and the Mendez v. Westminster decision. The phrase “for one” must clearly modify the first item only.

steps.title: Apply the comma-period rule for explanatory phrases
steps.content:
A comma sets off an explanatory phrase, and a period creates a sentence boundary.

The blank must include the year (1948), place a comma before “for one” to set it off, and then place a period after it to create a boundary before the independent clause.

Option A (“1948, for one. The”) keeps the year, comma, and phrase together as a unit, with the period separating it cleanly from “The Mendez v.

Westminster...” Option B breaks this apart with an early period, making “for one” seem to introduce the second event; option C omits the period entirely, using only commas; option D omits the year.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
